mission.title: Install and Configure Dragonfly
mission.description: Dragonfly is a modern in-memory data store that serves as a drop-in replacement for Redis and Memcached, offering significantly higher throughput and lower resource consumption. It is ideal for applications requiring fast data access and high performance. Key trade-offs include the need to ensure compatibility with existing Redis/Memcached clients and potential adjustments for optimal performance.

steps.title: Add the Dragonfly Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Dragonfly Helm chart repository to your Helm client, allowing you to install Dragonfly from it.

```bash

helm repo add dragonfly https://dragonflydb.github.io/charts

helm repo update

```

> This adds the Dragonfly Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for Dragonfly to function properly in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/v1.37.0/deploy/crds/dragonflydb.com_dragonflies.yaml

```

> This installs the Dragonfly CRDs.

steps.title: Install Dragonfly using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Dragonfly in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --create-namespace --version 1.37.0

```

> Installs Dragonfly in the 'dragonfly' namespace.

steps.title: Create a Dragonfly instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Dragonfly custom resource to deploy an instance of Dragonfly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

```

> Creates a Dragonfly instance named 'my-dragonfly'.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: dragonflydb.com/v1
kind: Dragonfly
metadata:
  name: my-dragonfly
  namespace: dragonfly
spec:
  replicas: 3
EOF

steps.title: Verify Dragonfly deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Dragonfly pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> Lists all pods in the 'dragonfly' namespace to verify they are running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Dragonfly
uninstall.description:
This step removes Dragonfly and its associated resources from the cluster. Be aware that this will result in data loss as all Dragonfly instances will be deleted.

```bash

helm uninstall dragonfly --namespace dragonfly

kubectl delete namespace dragonfly

```

> Uninstalls the Dragonfly Helm release.
> Deletes the Dragonfly namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Dragonfly
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your data. This process involves draining the pods, upgrading the Helm release, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <pod-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade dragonfly dragonfly/dragonfly --namespace dragonfly --version 1.37.0

kubectl get pods -n dragonfly

```

> Drains the pod to prepare for upgrade.
> Upgrades Dragonfly to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Dragonfly'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Dragonfly service is not running or the network policy is blocking access.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Dragonfly pods and ensure the service is correctly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Dragonfly instance not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Dragonfly custom resource was not created successfully.

**Fix:**
Verify the YAML configuration used to create the Dragonfly instance and reapply it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to schedule pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This happens when there are not enough resources available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in the Dragonfly deployment and adjust the cluster resources accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur due to various reasons including incorrect chart version or configuration errors.

**Fix:**
Review the Helm release logs for errors and ensure the correct chart version is being used.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for advanced caching strategies and improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Dragonfly pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n dragonfly'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
